_comment: STAGING-ONLY operator-declared node-identity map for the M7 window topology view (F1; see docs/TOPOLOGY_AND_DEVIANTS.md §3). It is the DEMO's source of human-legible node NAMES — and nothing else. The topology SHAPE, edges, and volumes are REAL observed traffic from the live engine; only these names are staging metadata. In production this map is the customer's OWN service registry / CMDB export (IP/CIDR/port -> name), or SPIFFE service names from the mTLS peer cert. The engine does NOT natively know service names; it knows hashed adjacency. THIS IS A DIFFERENT CONCERN from ground-truth-registry.json (the stagedlabel calibration ground truth) — leave that file untouched; the node resolver (internal/topology/identity) does not import the labeler. DISTINCT-IDENTITY SCHEME (no longer all-loopback 127.0.0.1): each mesh service now LISTENs on AND dials from its own 127.0.1.<K> address (K = port-8000: frontend 127.0.1.1 ... profile 127.0.1.16; see deploy/m7-window/server-compose.yml + mesh/main.go), so a service is named by IP ALONE — the IP-keyed entry coalesces its egress (src, port 0) and listen (dst, port N) sides into ONE node. The ingress gateway (Envoy) is the entry point: it accepts on 127.0.0.1:8080 and originates the connection to the frontend FROM 127.0.2.1 (envoy.yaml upstream_bind_config), so BOTH addresses are declared with the SAME name 'ingress-gateway' to coalesce into one ingress node. Service IPs here MUST match server-compose.yml's LISTEN addresses; callers here MUST match the simdriver's benign/attacker/recon identities (deploy/m7-window/sim-setup.sh + ground-truth-registry.json).
